{{Quote|AT-AT Swimmers currently blocking our advances on Kaal. Recommend importing attack subs from Mon Cala.|[[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]]|<ref name="IHB" />}}
The '''Aquatic Terrain Armored Transport''', also known as the '''AT-AT swimmer''', was a war vehicle used by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]], designed to be the aquatic equivalent of the land-based [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] [[Walker/Legends|walker]]. They were piloted by specially trained [[Vehicle Command Crewer|Imperial Army pilots]].

==Characteristics==
The AT-AT swimmer was manufactured in a joint venture between [[Kuat Drive Yards/Legends|Kuat Drive Yards]] and [[Hydrospeare Corporation]], and was derived from extreme modifications to the standard AT-AT that had been executed by the Imperial Army garrison on [[Kabaira]].<ref name="IHB">''[[Star Wars: Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]''</ref>

The command deck was attached to a broad area of the ship which contained passengers. It operated in deep sea operations alongside [[TIE fighter boat]]s and [[aquatic assault stormtrooper]]s and could be found in Imperial aquatic military bases. It could carry 40 troops, and its cargo capacity was 600 [[Kilogram/Legends|kilograms]].

Its shell-like armor was designed to withstand and protect the vehicle from the crushing pressures of deep [[Water/Legends|water]], the effects of salt and water, and [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] fire. Its head was capable of providing firing arcs in all directions.

The AT-AT swimmer had two modified heavy [[Laser cannon/Legends|laser cannons]], much like its land counterpart. Its main body was broader than that of the [[All Terrain Armored Transport/Legends|AT-AT]] to accommodate the four additional [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] engines that keep it afloat. These four repulsorlift engines produced a powerful field strong enough to repel any object on the craft's ventral section, but it could be penetrated by strong swimmers where it was non-existent.

==History==
===Galactic Civil War===
The basis for the eventual Aquatic Terrain Armored Transport was created via the AT-AT Swimmer, which had been extreme modifications to the mech that were ordered by the Imperial Army garrison on Kabaira. This eventually ended up mass-produced, with some eventually being placed on [[Kaal/Legends|Kaal]], which proved enough of a problem to the Rebel's efforts on the planet that [[General/Legends|General]] [[Carlist Rieekan/Legends|Carlist Rieekan]] made a note while reviewing the official field manual for the [[Imperial Military/Legends|Imperial Military]], ''[[Imperial Handbook: A Commander's Guide]]'', that they will need to import attack subs from Mon Calamari.<ref name="IHB" />
===Legacy===
By the time of [[Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire]], an [[AT-AT Swimmer (Darth Krayt's Galactic Empire)|updated model]] was used.
